tested_outcome: Production-ready first pass. Wallet hero landed cinematic and moody, anchor strikethrough clean, $5,799 dominant.

input_context.headline: DREADING YOUR NEXT POWER BILL?
input_context.headline_split: First half medium-weight, 'POWER BILL?' oversized in contrasting accent
input_context.solution_line: THE ANSWER: DUCTED A/C, FULLY INSTALLED, $5,799

assets_attached.instruction: Match style/density/direct-response feel, do NOT copy headline/hero/colours

composition_choices_that_worked: Wallet shot was photoreal, cinematic, slightly desaturated — financial pain conveyed without people in frame
Dramatic side-lighting on dark surface — coins falling out of empty wallet is the emotional hit
Headline split: 'DREADING YOUR NEXT' medium-weight + 'POWER BILL?' oversized accent = pulls eye correctly
Solution panel as vertical accent block on the right — creates clear problem/solution visual divide
WAS / NOW anchor structure: 'WAS $9,990' struck through, '$5,799' oversized — communicates discount + savings instantly
Bottom-left tilted burst badge for discount — feels stamped, not designed in
'Hurry — Limited Slots' italic urgency line under CTA
Did NOT specify dark/red palette — said 'pick fresh dark high-contrast palette' and AI chose well
full_prompt_used:
Use the attached image as a strong style and layout reference. Match its visual energy, density, and direct-response feel — but DO NOT copy its headline, hero visual, or specific colours. Generate fresh ones per the brief below.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for a residential ducted air conditioning installer in Brisbane, Australia.

MOOD & STYLE
Bold, dark, urgent, problem-led. This is a pain ad — it should stop the scroll with discomfort, then deliver a clear fixed-price solution. Choose a fresh dark high-contrast palette of your own with one strong accent colour for the solution/price elements. Pick aggressive bold display typography for the headline. All text must be unmissable at thumbnail size. Don't make it look like a stock template — it should feel direct-response, slightly aggressive, but professional.

LAYOUT (top to bottom)

1. HEADLINE — top third
The phrase splits into two parts:
   - "DREADING YOUR NEXT" in medium-weight uppercase
   - "POWER BILL?" oversized bold uppercase in a contrasting accent colour — this is the dominant element
Stack across two lines.

2. HERO VISUAL — middle, roughly 40% of canvas height
A photo-real close-up of an open empty wallet with coins falling out of it onto a dark surface. Dramatic side-lighting. Convey financial pain, money slipping away. No people in this shot. Cinematic, slightly desaturated, moody.

3. SOLUTION PANEL — right side or below the hero
A vertical solid-colour accent panel containing stacked text:
   - Small uppercase caps: "THE ANSWER:"
   - Medium uppercase: "DUCTED A/C, FULLY INSTALLED"
   - Above the price, smaller, struck through: "WAS $9,990"
   - Oversized bold dominant: "$5,799"

4. CTA BUTTON — beneath the solution panel
Rounded pill button reading: "LOCK IN YOUR PRICE"
Bold uppercase. High-contrast colour that pops against the dark background.

5. DISCOUNT BADGE — bottom-left or top-right corner
Circular burst badge reading: "40% OFF"
Bold contrasting colour, slightly tilted.

6. URGENCY LINE — small text under the CTA
Italic text: "Hurry — Limited Slots"

7. BRAND STRIP — very bottom edge
Thin horizontal strip with three brand logos evenly spaced and rendered in their native brand colours: Daikin, Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, Samsung. Use the real brand logos.

HARD RULES
- 1080×1080 aspect ratio (square), nothing else
- All text must be readable at thumbnail size on a phone
- Maximum 3 brand logos
- Do NOT add the words "0% interest" or "interest-free" anywhere
- Do NOT add any people, faces, or hands beyond what's in the wallet shot
- Do NOT add review badges or star ratings to this ad
- Do NOT include any dates, names, or specific identifying details
- Anchor price ($9,990) must be visibly struck through
lessons_for_layer_2: Pain ads work best with a non-person hero (wallet, bill, calculator) — abstract objects deliver emotion without uncanny-valley faces
Always describe the lighting and mood ('cinematic, slightly desaturated, moody') — that's how you avoid stock-photo aesthetic
Anchor + new price structure must be explicit: 'WAS $X struck through' vs '$Y oversized bold' — leave no ambiguity
Headline split into two parts (smaller setup + oversized payoff) creates eye flow — proven hierarchy pattern
When briefing dark palette, say 'dark high-contrast palette' not 'black background with red accents' — let AI pick the specific colours
